methods.description: This method involves presenting qualitative variables as percentages and quantitative variables as mean and standard deviation (SD), or median and interquartile range (IQR, Q1-Q3). A univariate analysis is performed to analyze differences between groups of study subjects allergic to cypress and olive (C+O group), and controls allergic to olive (O group) and cypress (C group) pollen.

steps.description: Qualitative variables were presented as a percentage and quantitative variables as mean and standard deviation (SD), or median and interquartile range (IQR, Q1-Q3).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate descriptive statistics for qualitative and quantitative variables

steps.description: A univariate analysis was performed to analyze differences between the groups of study subjects allergic to cypress and olive (C+O group), and controls allergic to olive (O group) and cypress (C group) pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: perform univariate analysis to compare groups

steps.description: Qualitative variables were compared with Pearson chi-squared test or Fisher´s exact tests and quantitative variables with KrusKall Wallis non-parametric test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform chi-squared, Fisher's exact, and Kruskal-Wallis tests

methods.description: This method involves using multiple correspondence analysis (MCA) and hierarchical classifications to discriminate groups of subjects. MCA is a multivariate technique to visualize the association among categorical variables through a graphic and was performed to study association between groups (C+O, C and O), symptoms (RC, asthma, seasonality) and allergen profile.

steps.description: Multiple correspondence analysis (MCA) and hierarchical classifications were conducted to discriminate groups of subjects.

MCA is a Multivariate technique to visualize the association among categorical variables through a graphic and was performed to study association between groups (C+O, C and O), symptoms (RC, asthma, seasonality) and allergen profile.
A dendogram plot (hierarchical cluster analysis, Ward´s method) was generated to classify patients of the C+O group with high degree of association.
steps.searchable_summary: generate dendogram plot using Ward's method

methods.description: This method describes the statistical software used for analysis and the significance level set for the study.

steps.description: Statistical analysis was performed with SPSS 17.0 (SPSS Inc., Chicago, IL, USA) and STATA 13.
steps.searchable_summary: use SPSS 17.0 and STATA 13 for statistical analysis

steps.description: Significant level was set at P<.05.
